statement: Per-run cleanup (`deleteRunData`) is the safest reset workflow for iterative canaries when preserving windows/evidence.
rationale: Global nukes remove windows; run-scoped delete aligns with desired preservation boundaries.
keyAssumptions: cleanup is run after terminal or intentionally aborted runs
validationCriteria: run artifacts and run telemetry are removed for targeted runs
critiquePoints: current implementation uses broad scans that may strain large datasets
questions: Do we need chunked/indexed delete for high-volume loops?
